mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 0.07% (near zero lower bound) signals monetary accommodation. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates, reducing 10-year Treasury yields as investors seek duration. The mechanism is always-on: lower policy rates mechanically push down term structure expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 20-day forward windows when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.7% signals labor market slack. Rising slack reduces wage pressure and inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as real rate expectations fall and inflation risk premium compresses. Mechanism activates when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 20-day yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 427,186M signals consumer demand strength. Strong demand sustains economic growth expectations, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as growth premium widens. The mechanism is always-on: higher sales directly correlate with stronger growth outlook and higher term yields.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 55% of 20-day windows despite RSAFS growth, or Rank IC between RSAFS and yield changes < 0.04 over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index at 101.03 combined with rising payroll employment (137,699K) indicates supply-side capacity utilization. High utilization triggers inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as inflation risk premium expands. Mechanism requires both INDPRO and PAYEMS to signal tightness simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.03 and PAYEMS at 137699K as of 2019-08-15
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (INDPRO AND PAYEMS) produces Rank IC < 0.06 compared to single-series baseline.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation at 234.93 (year-over-year ~1.7%) is below Fed's 2% target. Low inflation allows continued monetary accommodation, pushing 10-year Treasury yields lower as real rate expectations decline. Mechanism activates when CPI growth rate falls below 2.0% annualized.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between CPI growth and 20-day yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 880K combined with low FEDFUNDS (0.07%) signal monetary-driven housing demand. Strong housing demand sustains growth expectations and inflation, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher. Mechanism requires both accommodative policy and housing strength to activate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 880K and FEDFUNDS at 0.07% as of 2019-08-15
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with housing cycle recovery
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 versus single-series HOUST baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 137,699K combined with UNRATE at 6.7% signals labor market tightness despite elevated unemployment. This contradiction suggests structural labor market shifts reducing wage pressure, allowing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as inflation expectations remain anchored. Mechanism activates when PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE remains elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137699K and UNRATE at 6.7% as of 2019-08-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with structural labor market changes
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 compared to single-series UNRATE baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 15,932.9B (last quarterly observation) combined with declining RSAFS growth signals demand deceleration. Slowing growth reduces inflation expectations and long-term yield expectations, pushing 10-year Treasury yields lower. Mechanism activates when quarterly GDP growth falls below 2.0% annualized.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of triggered instances despite GDP deceleration, or Rank IC between GDP growth and 60-day yield changes < 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.
